validation_contract.goal: v6.3.0 (measured): run the pre-registered §6.2 micro-task bench live and commit the first real numbers; fix measurement integrity (review-yield dedupe, invalid archived record, void judge-number annotation); close the claims-vs-defaults gap (heterogeneity surfacing, gate-config-aware check-config, CI-anchor onboarding, outcome nudge); repair bench protocol rot (nonexistent --safe-mode flag, missing live tooling)
validation_contract.evidence: acceptance_criteria[] proving commands re-executed by run-evidence; suites green recorded in commands_run

independent_review.reviewer: codex (gpt family, codex-cli 0.144.1, fresh context) — cross-family from the opus implementation lanes
independent_review.notes: First pass: request_changes on both axes, 8 findings — headline: (F1) claiming the §6.2 rule fired violated §6.0's letter (every arm passed the objective battery), (F2) hybrid gate+model_routing configs slipped past check-config with routing validation skipped. All 8 fixed: outcome recorded as NOT claimed (fires:false) across results/README/CHANGELOG/ROADMAP with a future-runs-only §6.0 scope amendment; orchestration-marker detection in check-config + hybrid eval case; PROTOCOL JSON-shape wording hardened; artifacts_location recorded; install.py routing-host gating; CI example pinned @v6.3.0; truthful legacy-config message; --materialize requires explicit --out. Confirmation pass: functional approve; claims request_changes (2 wording mediums: heading still said 'the rule they fire'; 'pure tax' overgeneralized) — both fixed as prescribed; final claims-only check: approve. Reviewer ran the bench validator, check-config, git-diff §6 byte check, and traversal/materialize probes itself (read-only sandbox blocked the eval suites; those are green locally and gate CI).

security_review.reviewer: fable-5 orchestrator security pass (the dedicated security subagent failed on an API error; pass executed directly, distinct from the opus implementers)

findings.text: bench/runner.py materialize wrote task-spec file names without a traversal guard: a doctored spec with '../x' or an absolute path escaped --out
findings.resolution: fixed: absolute/parent-escaping names rejected (resolve + is_relative_to), regression-probed with relative and absolute escapes (both exit 1), independently re-probed by the codex confirmation pass

security_review.notes: Surfaces audited: materialize subprocess/copytree/shlex usage (list-arg git, no shell; quoted printed command); check-config gate path (no validation weakening; protect_harness/tests types enforced; hybrid configs now fail loudly); review-yield dedupe (no new I/O surface); committed results JSON (grep + scan-text clean: no secrets, no operator paths); installer next-steps (static strings, host from a known enum). Honest caveat: this pass was performed by the orchestrator, not a fresh-context subagent; the cross-family codex passes independently re-verified the traversal fix and results-file contents.

completion_record.goal: v6.3.0 (measured): run the pre-registered §6.2 micro-task bench live and commit the first real numbers; fix measurement integrity (review-yield dedupe, invalid archived record, void judge-number annotation); close the claims-vs-defaults gap (heterogeneity surfacing, gate-config-aware check-config, CI-anchor onboarding, outcome nudge); repair bench protocol rot (nonexistent --safe-mode flag, missing live tooling)
completion_record.right_size: minimal_new_code — edits/deletions to existing surfaces + the smallest live-bench enabler (materializer, results file); no new subsystems
completion_record.evidence: All seven suites green at 250 core + 37 add-on; bench validator OK on the committed live results (6 cells); npm CLI tests 33/0; check-config exit 0 on the repo gate-config; verify umbrella green modulo review attestation (this record).
completion_record.risks: Docs/counts drift (lint-pinned); §6.2 result is n=3 single-family single-task — CHANGELOG/README wording keeps claims scoped to the micro-task cell; materializer copies working-tree drop-in (operator must use a clean checkout for pristine provenance, documented in PROTOCOL).
completion_record.rollback: git revert the squash-merge commit; npm latest returns to 6.2.0 by publishing a follow-up release; no data migrations.
completion_record.follow_ups: Act on fired §6.2 rule (cut candidate); fix the three replicated field defects (pristine-gate waiver path, same-family fallback honesty, risk-tier consistency); discriminating tasks before Wave 4.3 (all in ROADMAP §Next).

control-report --review-yield no longer double-counts a release whose live record duplicates an archived record; an eval case pins the dedupe
Every archived record in docs/records/ passes check-record (v6.1.0 record repaired) and a lint keeps it true
check-config validates the three-key gate-config shape (exit 0 on this repo's own config) and heterogeneity enforcement status is surfaced loudly; routing suite pins it

Reality and memory suites stay green after gate changes

completion_record.done_when: All seven acceptance criteria proven by recorded passing commands; both reviews attested on the final diff; CI green; v6.3.0 released to npm; record archived.

minimality_decision.reason: Mostly edits/deletions to existing surfaces (dedupe in an existing query, record repair, doc annotations, one printed line, gate-config acceptance in the existing validator). The only new code is the smallest live-bench enabler: a workspace materializer subcommand on the existing runner and a committed results file. Bigger rungs (live orchestrator harness, new bench framework) rejected: the protocol explicitly keeps live runs an operator action.

plan: Lane A (opus): measurement integrity + defaults — review-yield dedupe + eval case; repair docs/records/v6.1.0-agent-record.json; archived-records check-record lint; README void-judge-number annotation; check-config accepts the three-key gate-config shape; heterogeneity enforcement status surfaced in brief/verify; installer next-steps add the CI anchor; SKILL.md outcome nudge. Rollback: revert lane commits.

Lane B (opus): bench enablement — PROTOCOL.md + 14-micro-bugfix.json isolation recipe updated to real claude CLI flags (--safe-mode is gone); runner.py gains a materialize subcommand for task workspaces; live-results assembly documented. Rollback: revert lane commits.

Orchestrator: run 6 live cells (3 seeds x {baseline,full}) under the amended recipe, score objectively, assemble+validate+commit bench/results/micro-bugfix-live-2026-07-21.json; README measured-cost paragraph; CHANGELOG/ROADMAP; version bump 6.3.0; independent cross-family review + security review; attest; PR; merge; GitHub Release -> npm; archive record.

review_findings.text: [codex F1] §6.2 'rule fired' claim violated §6.0's letter (all arms passed the objective battery)
review_findings.resolution: outcome recorded as NOT claimed (fires:false) in results/README/CHANGELOG/ROADMAP; §6.0 scope amendment for future runs only

review_findings.text: [codex F2] hybrid config (gate keys + model_routing, no profiles/steps) slipped through the gate-config shortcut skipping routing validation
review_findings.resolution: orchestration-marker detection added; hybrid configs take the full path and fail loudly; eval case extended

review_findings.text: [codex F3] PROTOCOL prescribed a single output-JSON shape; official docs and observed CLI disagree
[codex F4] results doc omitted the §5-required artifacts location
review_findings.resolution: artifacts_location added; raw per-cell artifacts preserved operator-local

review_findings.text: [codex F5] install.py recommended model routing setup for non-routing hosts (git/github)
review_findings.resolution: routing_hosts gating mirrors the npm CLI's showSetupModels

review_findings.text: [codex F7] legacy-config deprecation message overstated ('will stop being read' — the PreToolUse guard still reads it as a one-release fallback)
review_findings.resolution: message now states exactly which component still reads it

review_findings.text: [codex F8] --materialize without --out crashed on the fixture-filename default
review_findings.resolution: explicit --out required with a clear error

review_findings.text: [security] materialize task file names could escape --out via traversal/absolute paths
review_findings.resolution: fixed + probed (relative and absolute escapes exit 1)

review_findings.text: [codex confirmation] CHANGELOG heading still claimed the rule fired; 'pure tax' overgeneralized beyond the measured cell
review_findings.resolution: heading reworded; both cost passages scoped to the observed cell; final claims-only check approve

bugfix_test_waiver: The repaired defects are gate/measurement behaviors pinned by executable eval cases inside the suite runners (review-yield dedupe case in run_control_evals.py; archived-records check-record lint and count lint in run_evals.py; gate-config + heterogeneity cases), not by files under evals/cases/ that the tests.path_markers lexicon can see in the diff. The proving runs are recorded passing: python3 evals/run_evals.py and python3 evals/run_control_evals.py and python3 evals/run_reality_evals.py all pass at the new counts (250 core + 37 add-on).
